channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Congolese civilians caught in a growing rebellion face a terrible choice
item.description: GISENYI, Rwanda (AP) — Many of the millions of people trapped in eastern Congo's escalating rebellion face a terrible choice: Retreat into Congo's interior and seek the protection of a weak army in disarray, or cross into nearby Rwanda, which is accused of backing the rebels.
item.dc:creator: IGNATIUS SSUUNA and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge set to sentence former Sen. Bob Menendez, who was convicted of taking bribes of cash and gold
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez faces the possibility of a long prison term when he is sentenced Wednesday for selling his once-considerable clout in Washington for gold bars, a luxury car and hundreds of thousands of dollars in cash bribes.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Honda recalls more than 290,000 vehicles over possible engine issue
item.description: An error in the fuel injection electronic control unit could lead to the engine stalling or a loss os power.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails attempting to target Johnson, Hegseth, Bessent
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a man with Molotov cocktails and knives who was allegedly attempting to kill House Speaker Mike Johnson and President Trump’s two recently confirmed nominees, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Live updates: Democrats press RFK Jr. on past vaccine positions at fiery hearing
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Escaped North Carolina inmate has history of trying to flee law enforcement
item.description: An inmate who escaped the Craven County Jail in North Carolina over the weekend has a criminal history and previously has tried to flee police, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: California cities vow to ‘shut down’ EPA’s dumping site for ‘toxic’ waste
item.description: Four cities in Southern California are opposing an EPA processing site for "hazardous" waste from Los Angeles County's devastating Eaton Fire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man, 88, allegedly killed his wife, attempted to make killing look like a suicide
item.description: An 88-year-old Florida man is facing charges after allegedly killing his wife and staging the scene to make her death appear like a suicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims is in legal limbo as an unusual trial starts
item.description: Victims of a deadly 2023 Hawaii wildfire that decimated the town of Lahaina are expected to testify in court during an unusual trial to determine how to divide a potential $4 billion settlement.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: New results from a national exam find that America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Scores of Los Angeles residents returned Tuesday to wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community, the first opportunity most have had to carefully scour the sites of their former homes.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — President Donald Trump fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights law in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and CLAIRE SAVAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Young people 'inspired' by Trump and Musk, UCLA survey finds
item.description: (KTLA) -- A large number of young people say they are "inspired" by the likes of Elon Musk and Donald Trump and want to be like them, a survey from UCLA found.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC home to hundreds of thousands of undocumented people: Reports
item.description: The Trump administration is pushing to increase the arrests of undocumented people, issuing daily quotas for the tri-state area, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: California water regulators deny Trump's claim that US military 'turned on the water' in the state
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California water officials said this week there's no truth to President Donald Trump's assertion that the U.S. military has entered California and “turned on the water.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KRON4

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices are soaring. Don't expect that to change anytime soon
item.description: Bird flu is forcing farmers to slaughter millions of chickens a month, pushing U.S. egg prices to more than double their cost in the summer of 2023. And it appears there may be no relief in sight, given the surge in demand as Easter approaches.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, MARK VANCLEAVE and DEE-ANN DURBIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shooting at Indiana store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: ELKHART, Ind. (AP) — A shooting at a grocery store in northern Indiana on Monday left three people dead, including the suspected shooter, and two police officers wounded, officials said. Police received a 911 call at about 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter pardoned by Trump killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KTLA 5

item.title: PETA suggests replacing Punxsutawney Phil with a ‘weather reveal' cake
item.description: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals, or PETA, suggested that Groundhog Day be celebrated with a “weather cake” instead of pulling Punxsutawney Phil from his burrow to determine if winter will be prolonged or if spring will come early.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Super Bowl footballs are ready to go within hours of the matchup being set
item.description: As soon as this year's Super Bowl matchup was set, workers at the Wilson Sporting Goods football factory jumped into action. The factory in the rural village of Ada, Ohio, makes the game balls used by every NFL team along with many of the nation’s top college programs and high schools.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KRON4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: The only person in the world with a functioning pig organ is thriving after a record 2 months
item.description: An Alabama woman passed a major milestone Saturday to become the longest living recipient of a pig organ transplant – healthy and full of energy with her new kidney for 61 days and counting.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4
